element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fast travel between points of interest
Risk/reward trading routes with police patrols
Final mission arc in single-player campaign

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-driven rhythm with negotiable tension; safe docking periods alternate with optional combat encounters; single-player campaign provides structured narrative beats while multiplayer allows freeform tension management

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bulletin boards at space stations offer optional quests and jobs; players choose to engage or ignore bounty hunting and trading opportunities
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Asteroid and debris fields populate some systems; derelicts and secret hideouts (implied to exist but not detailed in source)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to flight controls, basic trading, tutorial combat encounters in controlled environment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Bounty hunting, multi-system exploration via jump gates, contraband smuggling with police patrols, expanded weapon/ship upgrades
difficulty_curve.late_game: Confrontation with mysterious alien force; advanced dogfighting against skilled adversaries; complex trading networks across 48 systems
notable_spikes: First jump gate transition (new territory pressure)
Introduction of police patrols (smuggling risk escalation)
Alien force encounter sequence (story campaign climax)

notable_level_patterns.description: Planetary bodies and space stations lie close to a single plane within each system, though some can be above/below plane; ships can travel out of plane creating 3D exploration space
Trade lanes form 'space highways' connecting points of interest (planets, stations, mining ops) to each other within a system; allows fast travel and safe passage
Systems are connected via jump gates; passing through gates unlocks new 48-system universe; gates serve as progression gates and fast travel points
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Transitions between Liberty, Bretonia, Kusari, Rheinland faction spaces

notable_level_patterns.description: Asteroid and debris fields populate some systems; require active piloting navigation (outside of trade lane safety)
Upon docking at stations/planets, interface changes from flight to point-and-click interaction mode; enables trading, NPC dialogue, quest pickup, ship upgrades
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any space station or planetary settlement
